back: An open standard for connecting AI applications to external tools and data - 'USB-C for AI'. Introduced by Anthropic in late 2024, now industry-wide.
N AI apps and M tools need N x M custom integrations without a standard. MCP reduces it to N apps + M connectors.
A small program on the tool side that announces capabilities ('list pull requests', 'create issue') and executes them when called.
No - the model still uses the normal tool loop. MCP standardizes how tools are discovered and connected: the plumbing, not the engine.

front: Name five things AI connects to via MCP
back: GitHub, Slack, databases, calendars, file systems (plus browsers, design tools, and thousands more).

front: Why do standards like MCP reduce lock-in?
back: Connectors work across AI apps, so you can switch providers and keep your integrations - like keeping chargers when you change phones.
